greeting.title: Passionate about creating amazing web experiences.

about.title: About Me
about.intro: I'm a code enthusiast with 15+ years of turning coffee into high-performance web applications. I don't have superpowers, but I love creating smooth user experiences like water. I've had the privilege of working with some pretty cool brands like Autodesk, General Motors, Frontier, Carnival Cruise, Telmex, Presidency of Mexico and Coca-Cola.

skills.design: Turn designer and product ideas into fully functional applications with a big eye on design.
skills.performance: Make fast performant websites with heavy traffic.

toolbox.description: The tools and technologies I use to build exceptional digital experiences
toolbox.hoverTip: Hover over any technology to see when I learned it and projects where I've used it

funFacts.title: Fun Facts
funFacts.languages: Fluent in English, Spanish, and JavaScript.
funFacts.power: Powered by code, coffee and music.
funFacts.music: Debugging soundtrack: Ambient music and John Coltrane.
funFacts.hobby: When not coding, you'll find me playing table tennis.

blog.title: Blog articles & tutorials
blog.description: I write about web development, design, and other tech-related topics. Here are some of my latest articles.

work.title: Work experience
work.description: I've had the privilege of working with different companies and agencies over the years, which has been a rich and learning experience.

companies.title: Disney
companies.description:
• Building two production Next.js and TypeScript applications: a customer support platform handling subscriber issues, payments, account history, and service changes at scale, and an internal integration platform that connects workflows and processes across teams throughout the company.
  • Both apps are heavily powered by AI, with multiple specialized chatbot agents working together to assist support agents, automate resolutions, and surface the right information at the right time, all wired up via Model Context Protocol (MCP) to tools like Jira, GitHub, LaunchDarkly, and Datadog.
  • Own the full feature lifecycle end-to-end: API design, UI, testing, feature flags, production monitoring, and CI/CD, keeping both platforms reliable and moving fast.

companies.title: SQOR.ai
companies.description:
• Joined a fast-moving AI analytics startup and worked end-to-end: built the APIs powering the intelligence loop, storing and serving outputs from multiple LLMs as they analyzed client data in real time and generated dynamic charts and business recommendations.
  • Designed and built personalized dashboard interfaces where visualizations and KPIs updated live based on AI discoveries, making complex data feel intuitive and accessible for non-technical users.
  • Brought structure and design leadership to a high-energy team: introduced cleaner development processes, took ownership of key UI/UX decisions, and helped transform ideas from whiteboard to production-ready features.

companies.title: Craigraphics
companies.description:
• Rebuilt ECUcomm's entire digital presence from scratch with a new design and modern stack (Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind, Sanity CMS), delivering dramatically better performance, SEO scores, and accessibility.
  • Handled everything end-to-end: wired up AWS SES for transactional email, set up CI/CD pipelines on Vercel, and handed the client a self-managed CMS so content updates never require a developer again.

companies.title: Autodesk
companies.description:
• Helped launch Autodesk's Profile app from day one, the single identity hub for 1M+ monthly users across the entire Autodesk ecosystem, built on React, GraphQL, and Node.js using micro-frontend architecture with a 30% improvement in load times.
  • Architected a scalable, reusable component system with TypeScript and Next.js that multiple teams could plug into, reducing duplicate work and keeping the product visually consistent at scale.
  • Built a shared React component library (Lerna + Rollup) that became the design foundation across teams, and connected the front-end to AWS Lambda and serverless infrastructure for backend flexibility.
  • Mentored junior developers on code quality, performance strategies, and modern engineering patterns.

companies.title: Frontier
companies.description:
• Led the redesign of Frontier's company blog from concept to launch, earning a finalist spot at the Best Blog Awards 2020 (US Social Media Awards at Pub-Con).
  • Built and deployed emergency alert pages under real pressure during severe weather events, delivering reliable critical communications for millions of customers.
  • Developed a YouTube-powered video gallery with social sharing integration, and collaborated across Commercial, Digital, and IT teams to keep digital initiatives moving.

companies.title: Carnival Cruise
companies.description:
• Built the front-end of an AngularJS kiosk app that cruise ship passengers used to browse movies, buy tickets, and redeem them at the onboard theater, bringing the full cinema experience to sea.
  • Led a small dev team and ensured the experience held up flawlessly across all screen configurations on board.

companies.title: 5M2
companies.description:
• Led front-end development on GOB.mx, Mexico's national government portal, helping bring bureaucratic processes online and making public services accessible to millions of citizens across the country.
  • Created a unified design system adopted by government agencies nationwide, standardizing the look, feel, and code of Mexico's entire public digital infrastructure.

companies.title: Telmex
companies.description: • Contributed to the full redesign of Telmex.com, one of the most-visited websites in Latin America, using Liferay, vanilla JS, jQuery, and SASS to serve tens of millions of users.

companies.title: Interalia
companies.description:
• Crafted rich, interactive digital campaigns for General Motors brands including Chevrolet, Buick, and Cadillac, creating immersive web experiences that made cars feel exciting long before social media took over.
  • Led micro-site and mobile campaign development for Coca-Cola Latin America, and won the redesign of the agency's own brand identity, selected from dozens of competing proposals.

contact.title: Lets connect
contact.description: My career has been a journey of continuous learning and growth, and I am always on the lookout for the next exciting challenge. Let's connect and see how we can build the future together.

form.error: An error occurred while sending the message. Please try again later.

projects.title: Highlighted Projects
projects.description: Here are some of the projects I've worked on.

apps.title: Profile Autodesk
apps.description: An user-centric approach that balanced functionality and aesthetic appeal. Utilizing Figma for design and adhering to Autodesk's brand guidelines, we engaged in continuous discussions to make informed decisions on the application's design and implementation. This close-knit collaboration ensured that both technical and visual aspects were seamlessly integrated, resulting in a robust and intuitive application that met the performance and usability needs of high-traffic users.

apps.title: Blog Frontier
apps.description: My creative solution for the project involved bringing the product owners' ideas to life with a strong focus on user experience. I was responsible for creating detailed user flows and wireframes, designing high-fidelity mockups, and leading a small development team to build the project. We developed a custom WordPress template tailored to our needs, ensuring a seamless and engaging user experience. Our efforts culminated in the blog becoming a finalist for Best Blog 2020 at the US Social Media Awards for Pub-Con, reflecting the success and impact of our work.
